chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-1908 of 2026 [Ahmed Shah V. Federation of Pakistan and others] Date Order with signature of Judge(s) Before: Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi 1. For order on office objection. 2. For hearing of CMA No.7020/2026. 3. For hearing of main case. Date of hearing and Order: 06.04.2026 Mr. Kausar Ali Shar, Advocate for the Petitioner. Ms. Wajiha Mahdi, D.A.G. Mr. Abdul Jaleel Zubedi, A.A.G. Mr. Khalid Langah, Mukhtiarkar, Darden, on behalf of Deputy Commissioner South-Respondent No.06. ********** O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. – The Petitioner filed this constitutional petition under Article 199 of the Constitution seeking a fair hearing regarding his nationality, production of record relating to his nationality/family history, protection against coercive action regarding his cattle farm/property, declaration that the impugned actions are illegal, and other ancillary reliefs. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner submitted that the Petitioner purchased the property through a registered Sale Deed; however, Respondent No. 6 pasted a notice on the property alleging that it belongs to an Afghan national and directing eviction within three days. It was further submitted that about 100 animals are present on the property which cannot be shifted within such a short time. 3. Learned A.A.G submitted a report stating that the Petitioner has been declared an Afghan national, therefore, such notice was issued and the property was sealed. 4. Prima facie, no material has been placed on record to substantiate such a claim; therefore, the matter is remanded to the Commissioner, Karachi, to decide the issue of sealing of the subject property within two weeks by speaking order, with cogent reasons and supporting documents whether the DC is competent to seal the private premises based on the analogy that petitioner is Afghan National, however, after hearing the Petitioner.

Learned A.A.G submitted a report stating that the Petitioner has been declared an Afghan national, therefore, such notice was issued and the property was sealed. 4. Prima facie, no material has been placed on record to substantiate such a claim; therefore, the matter is remanded to the Commissioner, Karachi, to decide the issue of sealing of the subject property within two weeks by speaking order, with cogent reasons and supporting documents whether the DC is competent to seal the private premises based on the analogy that petitioner is Afghan National, however, after hearing the Petitioner. 2 5. The petition, along with pending applications, stands disposed of in these terms. A compliance report shall be submitted through MIT-II. Let a copy of the order be communicated to the Commissioner Karachi for compliance. JUDGE JUDGE Ayaz Gul
